title: Analyzing Classification Errors
concept: Inspect model failures: why did it misclassify sarcasm or double negatives? Error analysis drives dataset improvements. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Preprocessing Pitfalls.'

exercises.prompt: When starting an error analysis, what should you focus on inspecting?
exercises.explain: Error analysis is all about looking at the incorrect predictions (failures) to understand the model's weaknesses.

cards.title: Why Did It Fail?
cards.body: Your AI model has a 90% accuracy rate. That sounds great! But what about the 10% it got wrong? Did it fail on tricky jokes, double negatives, or simple typos? Let's dive in and learn how inspecting our AI's mistakes helps us make it much smarter.

cards.title: What is Error Analysis?
cards.body: Error Analysis is the process of looking closely at the specific examples your AI model got wrong. Instead of just looking at a single accuracy percentage, you inspect the failures (like misinterpreting sarcasm) to figure out exactly why the AI made a mistake.

cards.title: The Game Tape Review
cards.body: Think of a model's errors like a soccer coach reviewing game tapes. Instead of just looking at the final score, the coach watches every missed pass and goal to see exactly what went wrong. Did the player slip, or did they misread the opponent? Analyzing errors helps you coach your AI to do better next time!

cards.title: The Sarcasm Trap
cards.body: Imagine you train a model to label movie reviews as positive or negative. The review says: "Oh great, another delayed sequel. Just what we needed!" The model labels it positive because of the words "great" and "needed". By doing error analysis, you discover your model cannot handle sarcasm!

cards.title: Myth: Just Make It Bigger
cards.body: It's tempting to think that if your model makes mistakes, you just need a bigger model. But often, the problem is that your training data is missing examples of sarcasm or complex language. Analyzing the errors tells you exactly what data you need to add!

cards.title: Scores vs. Details
cards.body: Compare how we look at model performance. Just looking at the score leaves you blind to specific issues, while inspecting errors gives you actionable insights.

left.text: tells you: 'Your model is 85% correct.' You don't know why the other 15% failed.

right.text: tells you: 'Your model fails whenever a sentence contains double negatives.' Now you know what to fix!

exercises.prompt: Why should you manually inspect the examples your model misclassified?

options.text: It automatically deletes the bad code for you.
It helps you find patterns in what the model struggles to understand.
It guarantees a 100% perfect model instantly.
It hides the errors so your clients don't see them.

exercises.explain: Looking at the actual failed examples reveals the specific linguistic patterns (like double negatives) that the AI doesn't understand yet.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A model's overall accuracy score tells you exactly which types of sentences (like sarcasm) it struggles with.
exercises.explain: Accuracy only tells you how often the model is right, not why it gets things wrong. You must inspect the errors to find patterns.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of a common classification error.
exercises.sentence: A model might label a sarcastic review as positive because it struggles to understand sarcasm.
exercises.explain: Sarcasm uses positive words to mean something negative, which easily tricks simple classification models.

exercises.prompt: Which AI response shows a classification error caused by failing to understand a double negative?

options.text: AI A: "I think this book is wonderful! 5 stars!" (Label: Positive)
AI B: "The acting was not bad at all." (Label: Negative)
AI C: "Where is the nearest movie theater?" (Label: Neutral)

exercises.explain: AI B is showing a lack of understanding of double negatives ("not bad"), which is a classic linguistic pattern discovered during error analysis. AI A is correct, and AI C is completely off-topic.

exercises.prompt: How does error analysis help us build better classification models?

options.text: By inspecting actual errors to find patterns and fix the training data.
By randomly changing the model's settings until it gets a higher score.
By deleting all the tricky sentences so the model never sees them.
By ignoring the errors and assuming the AI is always right.

exercises.explain: When an AI makes mistakes, it isn't random. By inspecting the specific examples it got wrong (like sarcasm or double negatives), we find patterns that tell us exactly how to improve our training data! Next up: Preprocessing Pitfalls.
